intro: Java's verbose, statically typed class hierarchy stands in sharp contrast to Python's dynamic duck-typing and concise syntax. Porting Java to Python means collapsing boilerplate: Java's getters/setters become plain properties, checked exceptions become try/except blocks, and Java's `ArrayList`/`HashMap` map directly to Python's built-in `list` and `dict`. Java's 10-line class definition often compresses to 3 lines of Python.

diffs.from: Every variable needs a declared type. Getters/setters are explicit methods.
diffs.to: Dynamic typing. Properties are plain attributes. 10 lines of Java often becomes 3.

diffs.from: JVM garbage collector. Objects allocated on heap explicitly via new.
diffs.to: CPython reference counting + cyclic GC. No new keyword.

diffs.from: ArrayList, HashMap, HashSet from java.util. Generics required.
diffs.to: list, dict, set built into the language. No import needed.

diffs.from: Checked exceptions must be declared with throws or caught.
diffs.to: All exceptions are unchecked. No throws declaration needed.

diffs.from: public static void main(String[] args) required in a class.

examples.title: Processing a list of objects
examples.description: Java ArrayList + Iterator loops condense to Python list comprehensions.
examples.toCode:
numbers = list(range(1, 9))

evens = [n * n for n in numbers if n % 2 == 0]
print(evens)  # [4, 16, 36, 64]

examples.title: Simple class definition
examples.description: Java's verbose POJO collapses to a Python dataclass or a short __init__ class.

tips.title: Eliminate getter/setter boilerplate
tips.body: Python attributes are public by default. Replace getName()/setName() with plain self.name. Use @property only when you need computed or validated access.

tips.title: ArrayList/HashMap → list/dict
tips.body: new ArrayList<String>() becomes []. new HashMap<String, Integer>() becomes {}. All operations (add, get, size) map to list.append, dict[key], len().

tips.title: Checked exceptions → try/except
tips.body: Remove throws declarations entirely. Wrap risky operations in try/except. Catch specific exception types: except ValueError, except IOError, etc.

tips.title: Static utilities → module functions
tips.body: Java static utility classes (StringUtils, MathHelper) become plain Python module-level functions. No class wrapper needed for utility logic.
